chunks.text: BEARS 2019: Nir Yosef - EECS at Berkeley BEARS 2019: Nir Yosef Lessons from the heterogeneity of immune cells Berkeley Annual Research Symposium (BEARS) 2019 Nir Yosef Biography Nir Yosef is an Assistant professor at the Department of EECS and a memb

ant professor at the Department of EECS and a member of the Center for Computational Biology at UC Berkeley. The overarching goal of his research is to utilize high-throughput genomic data sets, mostly based on DNA sequencing, in order to build mod

ly based on DNA sequencing, in order to build models that explain how gene expression is regulated. He is especially interested in immune cells, covering various aspects of their biology, such as their differentiation, stability/ plasticity, and re
eir differentiation, stability/ plasticity, and response to acute stimulation. < BEARS 2019: Rikky Muller Berkeley EECS Annual Research Symposium (BEARS) 2019 > View Open Faculty Positions
